summary: Suspicious package name and string-splitting obfuscation warrant manual review
details: Package name 'free-anthropic-claude' impersonates the Anthropic Claude brand and offers a 'free' variant, a common shape for credential-harvesting or proxy lures targeting developers searching for AI SDK access. lib/index.js line 178 contains a string-splitting construct (['ba','se','64'].join(...)) that reassembles the token 'base64' at runtime — a low-grade obfuscation technique used to hide encoding/decoding logic from naive scanners. Without traced execution evidence, the actual behavior of the package (whether it forwards prompts to an attacker-controlled endpoint, harvests provider API keys, or simply wraps a benign client) cannot be confirmed from the available signals. The combination of brand-impersonating name plus deliberate string fragmentation is sufficient to warrant human review before this package is approved for installer use.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
